name: The vulture's balcony – circular from Irurtzun
name_original: El balcón de los buitres – circular desde Irurtzun
region_intro: Hiking around Irurtzun, located in Navarre, Spain, offers diverse landscapes within the Larraun river valley. The region is characterized by prominent natural features such as the Peñas de Dos Hermanas rock formations and extensive beech forests, particularly around the Trinidad de Ergo shrine. Trails often traverse a mosaic of agricultural land and pastures, providing varied terrain for exploration. Nearby karst massifs also contribute to the varied geological features. Best hiking trails around Irurtzun The most popular hiking route is The vulture's balcony – circular from Irurtzun, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This easy route offers panoramic views and opportunities to observe griffon vultures. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aixita Trail, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail provides views of the Larraun river and the Aixita Castle Ruins. Local hikers also love the Monte Erga Summit – Ermita de la Trinidad loop from Irurtzun, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through beech forests and offering views of the Monte Erga summit, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Irurtzun is defined by the Larraun River valley, the distinctive Peñas de Dos Hermanas, and enigmatic beech forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Irurtzun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Irurtzun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Irurtzun?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Irurtzun, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Irurtzun?
A: Hikes in Irurtzun offer a varied landscape, from the scenic Larraun river valley to the distinctive rock formations of Peñas de Dos Hermanas. You'll traverse enigmatic beech forests, agricultural lands, and pastures, with some routes also touching on karst massifs.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Irurtzun?
A: Yes, Irurtzun offers several easy routes. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970695132/the-vulture-s-balcony-circular-from-irurtzun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vulture's balcony – circular from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3-mile (4.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 41 minutes, offering panoramic views and opportunities to observe griffon vultures. The Plazaola Greenway also provides an accessible, relatively flat option.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Irurtzun?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Irurtzun are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970695132/the-vulture-s-balcony-circular-from-irurtzun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vulture's balcony – circular from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Erga Summit – Ermita de la Trinidad loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to look out for on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy stunning panoramas from the Peñas de Dos Hermanas, often referred to as "The Balcony of the Vultures," where you might spot griffon vultures. The enigmatic beech forests, particularly around the Trinidad de Ergo shrine, are also a highlight. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vultures' Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for exceptional vistas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, hikers can discover several historical and cultural points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3552575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixita Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, a fortified promontory from the Middle Ages, offers historical intrigue. The Trinidad de Ergo shrine is another notable religious site nestled within the beech forests. The Gulina Viaduct, with its 17 stone arches on the Plazaola Natural Trail, is also an impressive sight.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near Irurtzun that I can visit?
A: Yes, there are beautiful waterfalls in the vicinity. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artazul Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4107585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ixkier Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both offering picturesque natural settings for a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Irurtzun?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to beech forests, and the stunning panoramic views, especially from spots like the Peñas de Dos Hermanas.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Irurtzun?
A: Irurtzun is a well-connected town in Navarre. While specific public transport routes to individual trailheads can vary, local bus services typically connect Irurtzun with larger towns, from which you might access various starting points. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Certainly. For those seeking a greater challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Trinidad – PuenteLarraun loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 12.6-mile (20.3 km) trek with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 7 hours to complete.

Q: What is the Plazaola Greenway and how can I access it from Irurtzun?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a former railway line converted into a scenic route for hiking and cycling. It largely follows the Larraun River and is easily accessible from Irurtzun. The section from Irurtzun to Sarasa is approximately nine kilometers and offers an easy, picturesque path through diverse landscapes.
tips: Also known as "balcony of the vultures" its name is due to the fact that from this colorful panoramic point it is possible to see these birds. The arrival trail runs through a splendid natural landscape created by the erosion of the Larraun River. From the viewpoint you will have some beautiful postcards towards the Sakana corridor and the Irurtzun valley to the city of Pamplona/Iruña.
